events.title: Intro to 3D Printing: Beginner Workshop (Evening)
events.subtitle: Get started with 3D printers after class
events.short_description: Beginner-friendly evening workshop covering 3D printer basics, safety, and how to submit your first print.
events.description:
This beginner-friendly workshop introduces you to the 3D printers available in the library makerspace. We will cover basic safety, how to prepare simple models for printing, approved materials, and how to submit your first print job to the queue.

No prior experience is required. This session is designed especially for students who have never used a 3D printer before.

Topics include:
- 3D printer safety and policies
- Supported file formats and where to find starter models
- Basic slicing settings (layer height, infill, supports)
- How to submit a print request through the library system

You’ll leave with the knowledge needed to safely and successfully request your first 3D print.

events.title: Intro to 3D Printing: Beginner Workshop (Late Session)
events.subtitle: Second-chance evening 3D printing intro
events.short_description: A repeat beginner 3D printing workshop for students who can’t attend earlier in the month.
events.description:
Missed the first intro? This late-session beginner 3D printing workshop covers the same core content: safety, file preparation, and how to submit prints through the makerspace.

We’ll walk through:
- Makerspace 3D printing policies
- Finding and downloading simple models
- Basic slicing settings using our supported software
- Submitting and tracking your print jobs

This session is ideal for students who want a refresher or couldn’t attend the first evening workshop.

events.title: Skill-Building Lab: Intro to Vector Design
events.subtitle: Morning design skills for makers
events.short_description: Learn basic vector design skills for use with laser cutters, vinyl cutters, and other makerspace tools.
events.description:
Develop core digital design skills in this hands-on skill-building lab focused on vector graphics. We’ll introduce the basics of working with paths, shapes, and text using free software tools.

This session is ideal preparation for working with our laser cutter, vinyl cutter, and CNC tools, and is appropriate for beginners.

You’ll practice:
- Setting up documents for fabrication
- Creating simple shapes and logos
- Preparing designs for export to makerspace equipment

All software is provided on lab computers; you may optionally bring your own laptop.

events.title: Skill-Building Lab: Rapid Prototyping Techniques
events.subtitle: Turn ideas into quick physical prototypes
events.short_description: Hands-on skill-building session exploring simple rapid prototyping methods using cardboard, foam, and 3D-printed components.
events.description:
In this mid-day skill-building lab, you’ll learn rapid prototyping techniques that help you quickly test ideas before investing in final materials.

We’ll cover:
- Low-fidelity prototyping with cardboard and foam
- Using 3D-printed connectors and brackets
- Basic techniques for cutting, scoring, and fastening
- Documenting prototypes for class projects and portfolios

This workshop pairs well with our Intro to Vector Design session earlier the same day, but you may attend either independently.
events.location_details: Library Lower Level, Open Collaboration Zone

events.title: Skill-Building Lab: Documentation & Project Photos
events.subtitle: Improve how you capture and share your maker projects
events.short_description: Afternoon skill-building session on photographing and documenting makerspace projects for class, competitions, and portfolios.
events.description:
Good documentation is critical for sharing your makerspace work. In this session, you’ll learn practical tips for photographing projects, organizing build notes, and presenting your process.

We’ll discuss:
- Simple lighting and background setups
- Using your phone camera effectively
- Capturing in-progress photos and videos
- Writing short project blurbs and captions

Bring a small project if you’d like practice photos taken during the workshop.

events.title: Laser Cutter Safety Orientation (Friday Morning)
events.subtitle: Required training for independent laser cutter use
events.short_description: Learn essential safety procedures and policies required before reserving the makerspace laser cutter.
events.description:
Before you can reserve and operate the makerspace laser cutter independently, you must complete this safety orientation.

This session covers:
- Laser cutter safety hazards and emergency procedures
- Approved and prohibited materials
- Ventilation and fire safety
- Job setup, focusing, and supervised first cuts

Completion of this orientation will be recorded on your makerspace account and is required to book future laser cutter time.

events.title: Laser Cutter Safety Orientation (Friday Afternoon)
events.subtitle: Afternoon safety session for laser cutter access
events.short_description: Safety orientation required to gain swipe access and reserve the makerspace laser cutter.
events.description:
This afternoon session offers the same required content as our morning laser cutter safety orientations, with a later time to fit more schedules.

You’ll learn:
- Laser cutter operating procedures and safe workflows
- Material selection and testing
- How to avoid common mistakes that damage equipment
- How to schedule laser cutter reservations after completing orientation

Attendance will be recorded; successful completion grants you access to book laser cutter time.

events.title: Laser Cutter Safety Orientation (Friday Early Session)
events.subtitle: Start your Friday with required laser safety training
events.short_description: Morning laser cutter safety orientation covering safe use, materials, and booking policies.
events.description:
This Friday morning laser cutter safety orientation is ideal for students who want to complete requirements before weekend project work.

Session topics include:
- Machine startup and shutdown procedures
- Fire safety and emergency stop usage
- Approved thickness limits and test cuts
- How to reserve future laser cutter time after orientation

This orientation is required before you can independently reserve the laser cutter.

events.title: Arduino Workshop: Blinking LEDs & Basics
events.subtitle: First steps with Arduino programming
events.short_description: Hands-on workshop introducing Arduino boards, programming basics, and simple LED projects.
events.description:
Curious about Arduino but not sure where to start? In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll learn how to connect an Arduino board, write simple sketches, and build your first blinking LED circuit.

We’ll cover:
- What Arduino is and how it’s used in projects
- Installing and using the Arduino IDE
- Building a basic LED circuit on a breadboard
- Modifying example code to change blink patterns

No prior coding or electronics experience is required. All materials and boards are provided for use during the workshop.

events.title: Arduino Workshop: Sensors & Serial Monitor
events.subtitle: Add sensors to your Arduino projects
events.short_description: Intermediate Arduino workshop exploring basic sensors and data output using the serial monitor.
events.description:
Build on your Arduino skills by adding simple sensors to your projects. In this intermediate workshop, you’ll connect common sensors and learn how to read their data in the Arduino serial monitor.

We’ll work through:
- Wiring and reading a light sensor
- Using a temperature sensor with sample code
- Printing sensor values to the serial monitor
- Basic troubleshooting tips for sensor circuits

Some prior Arduino experience (such as the Blinking LEDs workshop) is recommended. All components are provided during the session.

events.title: Open Lab: Weekend Makers Block (Saturday)
events.subtitle: Drop-in project time for individuals and groups
events.short_description: Reserve a 4-hour Saturday open lab block to work on personal, class, or club projects using makerspace equipment.
events.description:
Use this extended Saturday Open Lab block to make progress on your personal builds, class assignments, or student organization projects. During Open Lab, core makerspace tools are available on a first-come, first-served basis, with staff on hand for basic guidance.

Groups (such as student clubs) may reserve this block for collaborative work. Some equipment (like the laser cutter and 3D printers) may still require prior training and separate reservations.

Please arrive within the first hour of the block; unclaimed reservations may be released to walk-in users.
events.location_details: Library Lower Level, Makerspace Main Floor

events.title: Open Lab: Weekend Makers Block (Sunday)
events.subtitle: Sunday afternoon open lab access
events.short_description: Three-hour Sunday Open Lab session for independent and group work in the makerspace.
events.description:
This Sunday Open Lab block provides flexible time to continue projects, experiment with tools, or collaborate with classmates.

Staff will be available to help you get oriented to equipment and locate materials. Some tools still require prior completion of safety orientations or equipment trainings.

Groups are welcome; please indicate your group name and size when making a reservation.
events.location_details: Library Lower Level, Makerspace Main Floor

events.title: Family Craft Night: LED Paper Lanterns
events.subtitle: Evening crafting for kids and families
events.short_description: A family-friendly crafting event where you’ll build glowing paper lanterns using simple LED circuits.
events.description:
Bring the family to the makerspace for a fun evening of crafting and simple electronics. In this guided activity, families will design and assemble paper lanterns with battery-powered LEDs.

This event is designed for children ages 7+ with an adult caregiver. No prior experience is needed; all materials and tools are provided.

We’ll cover:
- Basic circuit concepts using coin-cell batteries and LEDs
- Safe use of craft tools (scissors, hole punches, tape)
- Simple decoration ideas for custom lantern designs

Please register once per family and indicate the number of participants on the form.

events.title: Equipment Training: 3D Printer Level 1
events.subtitle: Required training for basic 3D printer access
events.short_description: Hands-on equipment training covering operation and safety for the makerspace’s basic 3D printers.
events.description:
Complete this Level 1 equipment training to gain access to our standard FDM 3D printers. This session focuses on safe operation and the specific procedures used in our makerspace.

Topics include:
- 3D printer components and controls
- Loading and unloading filament
- Starting, pausing, and stopping prints
- Makerspace-specific safety rules and policies

After successfully completing this training, your account will be updated to allow you to book 3D printer time.
events.location_details: Library Lower Level, Room 101 (3D Printing Area)

events.title: Equipment Training: 3D Printer Level 2
events.subtitle: Advanced settings and troubleshooting
events.short_description: Follow-up 3D printer equipment training focused on advanced slicer settings and troubleshooting failed prints.
events.description:
This Level 2 equipment training is recommended for users who have completed 3D Printer Level 1 and want to improve print quality and reliability.

We’ll explore:
- Advanced slicing settings (supports, infill patterns, speed)
- Common print failures and how to avoid them
- Best practices for bed adhesion and calibration
- Material-specific tips (PLA, PETG, and more)

This session is optional but strongly encouraged for frequent 3D printer users.
events.location_details: Library Lower Level, Room 101 (3D Printing Area)

events.title: Equipment Training: Sewing Machine Basics
events.subtitle: Learn to safely use the makerspace sewing machines
events.short_description: Beginner equipment training on threading, basic stitches, and safe operation of sewing machines.
events.description:
Get certified to use the makerspace sewing machines. This beginner equipment training covers safe operation and basic sewing skills suitable for craft, costume, and soft-circuit projects.

You’ll learn:
- Sewing machine parts and basic maintenance
- Threading the machine and winding bobbins
- Selecting stitches and adjusting tension
- Safety guidelines and troubleshooting common issues

After successfully completing this training, you’ll be able to reserve sewing machines for independent use.

events.title: Equipment Training: Sewing Machines for Wearables
events.subtitle: Prepare for soft circuits and wearable projects
events.short_description: Intermediate sewing machine training with a focus on projects that integrate electronics and wearables.
events.description:
This intermediate training builds on Sewing Machine Basics and focuses on techniques used in wearable projects and soft circuits.

We’ll cover:
- Working with conductive thread and specialty fabrics
- Planning seams and channels for embedded electronics
- Reinforcing stress points for long-lasting wearables

Prior sewing machine experience (such as Sewing Machine Basics) is recommended.

events.title: Virtual Portfolio Workshop: Building a Maker Showcase
events.subtitle: Create a digital portfolio of your makerspace projects
events.short_description: Online workshop on organizing, photographing, and presenting makerspace projects in a digital portfolio.
events.description:
Join this virtual workshop to learn how to turn your makerspace projects into a polished online portfolio. Whether you’re preparing for internships, grad school applications, or competitions, a strong portfolio can help you stand out.

We’ll discuss:
- Choosing which projects to feature
- Photographing and recording your work effectively
- Writing concise project summaries
- Platforms and tools for hosting portfolios

Participants are encouraged to have a few project photos or drafts ready to share, but it’s not required.
events.location_details: Online via Zoom (link emailed 24 hours before the event)

events.title: Advanced Digital Fabrication Project Studio
events.subtitle: Capstone-style advanced makerspace workshop
events.short_description: Advanced workshop for experienced makers to plan and execute a complex digital fabrication project. Prerequisite events required.
events.description:
This advanced, studio-style workshop is designed for experienced makers who want structured support completing a substantial digital fabrication project (for example, an exhibition piece or competition entry).

During this multi-hour session, you will:
- Present a brief project proposal
- Develop a fabrication plan using multiple tools (e.g., laser cutter, 3D printer, CNC, textiles)
- Work through build steps with staff feedback
- Capture high-quality documentation suitable for a portfolio

PREREQUISITES:
Participants are expected to have completed at least one vector design and one prototyping-focused skill-building event prior to attending (see the Prerequisite Events section for recommended sessions). This helps ensure you are ready to focus on project execution rather than introductory skills.

This workshop is intended for advanced users; space is limited to allow for individualized feedback.

events.title: Laser Cutting Project Clinic
events.subtitle: Get help preparing files for the laser cutter
events.short_description: Bring your designs and get one-on-one help preparing files and choosing materials for laser cutting projects.
events.description:
This informal project clinic is for makers who have already completed the Laser Cutter Safety Orientation and are ready to work on specific projects.

Drop in during the scheduled time to:
- Get feedback on your vector files
- Choose appropriate materials and settings
- Learn tips for engraving vs. cutting
- Troubleshoot issues from previous jobs

This clinic does not replace the required safety orientation. Please bring your design files (or a laptop/USB drive) if you have them.
